title: Day 21: Connection Week — What You've Rebuilt

steps.title: The Heart of Your Marriage
steps.body:
Three weeks ago, you started this journey with diagnosis. You looked honestly at where your marriage stands.

Week 2, you learned to repair — to mend what's broken and build skills for hard conversations.

This week, you did something different. You rebuilt connection. Not by fixing problems — by creating warmth.

Let's name what you've built:

• Day 15 — You recommitted to friendship at the center of your marriage
• Day 16 — You went deeper into emotional intimacy and vulnerability
• Day 17 — You brought play and lightness back into your relationship
• Day 18 — You reclaimed physical touch as a language of connection
• Day 19 — You created rituals that anchor your relationship
• Day 20 — You understood how trust is built in tiny moments, not grand gestures

That's not a to-do list. That's a different way of being together.

Diagnosis gave you clarity.
Repair gave you skills.
Connection gave you warmth.

Without connection, a marriage survives.
With it, a marriage thrives.

questions.label:
Reflect on this week:

• What moment felt most connecting?
• What was hardest?
• Where did you resist — and why?
• What surprised you?

Write honestly. This is your progress report to yourself.

steps.question: What's the connection practice you most want to carry forward?
options: Vulnerability — sharing more of my inner world
Play — keeping lightness and fun alive
Non-sexual touch — staying physically connected without pressure
Rituals — keeping our small anchors in place
feedback: Vulnerability is the deepest connector. Keep climbing those rungs — even when it's scary. Your marriage gets richer as you do.
Play is the unsung hero of long-term relationships. Never let adulthood fully kill your silliness together.
Non-sexual touch is the foundation. Keep hugging, keep holding hands, keep staying close with no agenda.
Rituals are what hold against the storm. Keep at least one anchor — even just a daily check-in or a six-second kiss.

steps.title: Three Weeks In — Where Are You?

questions.label:
Three weeks ago, on Day 1, you rated your marriage. Do you remember what you said?

If things feel different now, how? Even small shifts count.

• Can you name one conversation you've had that you wouldn't have had three weeks ago?
• Has one habit shifted?
• Is there one area where you feel just slightly more hopeful?

Write your progress. Acknowledge it. This matters.

steps.title: What's Coming: Mission Week
steps.body:
You've diagnosed. You've repaired. You've connected.

Week 4 is where it all comes together. Mission Week is about designing your shared future.

This week you'll:
• Define your marriage mission — what are you building together?
• Align on your shared vision for the next year, five years, and beyond
• Create an action plan that turns insight into lasting change
• Celebrate how far you've come in 28 days

Three weeks of building. One week of launching.

steps.title: Before and After
steps.body:
Think about where you were 21 days ago vs now.

Then: You knew things could be better but weren't sure where to start.
Now: You have language for your patterns. You've practiced repair. You've felt more connected.

Then: Hard conversations felt dangerous.
Now: You have a do-over. You have soft startups. You know how to listen without defending.

Then: Connection was something you hoped for.
Now: You have rituals that build it daily.

That's not trivial. That's transformation in three weeks.

One more week to go. The last week is where you decide what kind of marriage you're building — together, on purpose.

steps.title: Thank Your Partner
steps.prompt:
Write a short message to your partner — not a text, actually write it down or say it — expressing gratitude for this journey.

'Thank you for showing up these three weeks. I know it hasn't always been easy. I see you trying. And I'm grateful to be doing this with you.'

That's it. No requests, no feedback, no 'wouldn't it be great if.' Just gratitude for showing up.
steps.body:
Write a short message to your partner — not a text, actually write it down or say it — expressing gratitude for this journey.

'Thank you for showing up these three weeks. I know it hasn't always been easy. I see you trying. And I'm grateful to be doing this with you.'

steps.title: Achievement Unlocked: The Connector
steps.body:
You completed Connection Week. Not by reading about connection — by practicing it. Vulnerability, play, touch, rituals, trust. You've built a marriage that's not just functional but warm.

Badge earned: The Connector

steps.title: Day Complete
steps.body: Great work today! You're building something real.
